docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/: 5342-568c-b881-4b2a & b1a4-af7a-4a48-465d (no ordering edge)

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a covering AC (or extend AC2/AC3) to require that any re-run triggered by the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE is committed as a docs/experiments/ writeup recording the changed method and the re-run result, not just the final deferral.
evidence: Context: 'an experiment that fails its own validity gate... gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method; if still not clearly passing, every dependent R-story closes deferred with the results recorded'
AC3: 'closed/deferred with the failing result recorded' — covers only the final deferred state, not the intermediate re-run documentation
findings.finding: The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE described in Context requires 'at most ONE documented re-run with changed method' before deferral, but no AC verifies that this intermediate re-run is actually documented in the repository. AC3 only requires that a deferred R-story has 'the failing result recorded' — it does not require the re-run attempt and its changed method to be committed.
findings.location: Context (INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE paragraph) vs. Acceptance Criteria AC3
scenarios: An inconclusive experiment triggers a re-run; the re-run is conducted informally without a committed writeup; the R-stories are then deferred with only the final result recorded, losing the re-run evidence and method-change rationale.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2 to: 'Each experiment child is closed with its results committed as a docs/experiments/ writeup; if the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE triggers a re-run, the re-run writeup (including changed method) must be committed before dependent R-stories are deferred.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] R5 AC4 should be amended to remove the conditional: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md and docs/event-schema.md updated to reflect the new sub-answer and sidecar schema change.'
evidence: R5 What: 'Add the asserted_capability_confirmed sub-answer to the review_kernel pass-2 verification vocabulary'
R5 Scope: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py (version bump + lossless persistence of the new sub-answer)'
R5 AC4: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md + docs/event-schema.md (if sidecar shape changes) updated'
The sub-answer addition to sidecar.py is the definition of R5's change; the condition always evaluates to true; writing it as conditional creates an apparent escape hatch
findings.finding: R5's AC4 conditions the docs/event-schema.md update on 'if sidecar shape changes,' but the sidecar shape change is definitionally certain (a new sub-answer is always added when R5 lands), making the documentation update a mandatory step that is syntactically optional.
findings.location: child ea39-c2d1-e26a-4ee6 (R5) AC4
scenarios: A reviewer approves R5 without updating docs/event-schema.md, citing the 'if' as allowing deferral; tooling that parses sidecars or the event schema breaks silently in production
findings.suggested_fix: Change R5 AC4 to: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md and docs/event-schema.md are updated in the same change to document the new asserted_capability_confirmed sub-answer and its na-outside-cohort constraint.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE, enumerate the valid axes for 'changed method' (e.g. 'changed method means at least one of: different annotator pool, revised annotation guide addressing the specific disagreement category, or larger sample drawn from a different corpus slice') to make the re-run eligibility check evaluable.
evidence: Plan Context text: 'an experiment that fails its own validity gate (e.g. E1 kappa < 0.7) or returns an ambiguous result gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method'
ABSENCE: no definition of what 'changed method' means — no enumeration of valid method-change axes or examples that would make this rule evaluable
The rule directly gates R-story closure decisions but provides no criteria for distinguishing a valid 'changed method' re-run from an invalid one
findings.finding: The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE's 'at most ONE documented re-run with changed method' is a decision protocol with an unspecified 'changed method' vocabulary. The rule controls whether a dependent R-story closes deferred or may proceed, but 'changed method' has no defined axes (e.g. sample size change, annotation protocol change, judge pool change, scoring-window change). Without this, the re-run eligibility check is unactionable.
findings.location: Context section — 'INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: an experiment that fails its own validity gate…gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method; if still not clearly passing, every dependent R-story closes deferred'
scenarios: E1 returns kappa = 0.62 (below 0.7). A re-run is attempted. The question 'was the method sufficiently changed to count as a valid retry?' has no answer because 'changed method' is not defined — an executor might change the sample size by 1 and claim the retry is valid.
Two R-story authors disagree on whether a re-run qualifies as a 'changed method', blocking the deferral decision.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE (one re-run limit, defer-on-ambiguity, no rounding up) to docs/plan-review-gate.md or a dedicated ADR so it is a durable repo artifact independent of the epic ticket.
evidence: 'INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: an experiment that fails its own validity gate (e.g. E1 kappa < 0.7) or returns an ambiguous result gets at most ONE documented re-run with changed method; if still not clearly passing, every dependent R-story closes deferred with the results recorded - ambiguity never rounds up to a pass.'
Absence: no mention of committing this rule to docs/plan-review-gate.md or a dedicated ADR
findings.finding: The INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE (one re-run allowed, then defer with results recorded) is a critical operational procedure governing the entire R1-R7 pipeline, but it lives only in this epic's Context section — not in any committed repository artifact. If the epic ticket becomes inaccessible or the rule needs to be applied by someone without ticket context, there is no doc to consult.
findings.location: Context: 'INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: an experiment that fails its own validity gate...'
scenarios: A future experiment returns inconclusive results; the engineer handling it has no committed doc to consult for the one-re-run rule and either re-runs indefinitely or rounds ambiguity up to a pass.
The epic ticket is archived or migrated and the rule is lost, silently removing a safeguard against optimistic-bias in experiment evaluation.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Experiment validity rules' section to docs/plan-review-gate.md reproducing the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE verbatim, referenced from each child experiment story's AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] E2's README pre-registration for the 'unnecessary mechanism' class must explicitly cite R4's >=8/10 threshold as the consuming gate row, and R4's AC must confirm it matches the E2-pre-registered value; the two children should cross-reference to prevent independent threshold drift.
evidence: E2 AC2: 'Thresholds are pre-registered in the README in a commit that precedes any detector run against the corpus'
E2 What: 'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table'
R4 AC1: '>=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants with <=1/10 controls flagged'
The parent epic does not spell out the per-class gate-table numbers in its AC; it defers to 'the report's section-6 experiment table' and says 'the child ACs are the authoritative gate rows'
If R4's AC is authoritative at >=8/10, E2's README must pre-register the same threshold; the two children do not cross-reference each other to confirm this
findings.finding: E2 pre-registers detection thresholds from 'the epic's gate table' for all 5 defect classes including 'unnecessary mechanism,' but R4 independently states its gate as '>=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants' - if E2's pre-registered threshold for class 4 differs from R4's stated >=8/10, there is a threshold conflict where E2 records a pass/fail that does not match R4's consuming gate row.
findings.location: child d125-b4ce-c57f-4f06 (E2) AC2; child 03a9-e6d1-4acc-435d (R4) AC1
scenarios: E2 pre-registers unnecessary-mechanism class at >=7/10 from the report table; the detector achieves 7.5/10; E2 marks it as a pass; R4's AC requires >=8/10 which the detector also achieved, so this specific case is fine — but if E2 pre-registers at >=9/10 and detector achieves 8/10, E2 records a fail while R4's gate row would pass
Implementers use E2's pre-registered threshold as definitive and overlook R4's stated threshold, creating an undocumented discrepancy
findings.suggested_fix: Add to E2's scope: 'Pre-registered thresholds are drawn from child ACs (authoritative per epic): unnecessary-mechanism class threshold = >=8/10 (R4 AC1).' Add to R4's AC1: 'The >=8/10 threshold must match the E2 pre-registered value for this class.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit R6 carve-out to the Success Criteria first bullet (e.g., 'every implemented recommendation except R6, whose gate terms are ship-first per AC3') so the section is self-consistent without requiring cross-referencing Context.
evidence: Success Criteria: 'Every implemented recommendation has a committed docs/experiments/ writeup showing its gate row passed before implementation'
Context: 'R6 is the one deliberate exception: a deterministic pass-4 coaching move with no detection behavior ships first with no eval gate (validated by dogfooding via R7 metrics)'
AC3: 'R6's terms are ship-first: deterministic rendering + validator compliance'
findings.finding: Success Criteria says 'Every implemented recommendation has a committed docs/experiments/ writeup showing its gate row passed BEFORE implementation,' but Context and AC3 make R6 an explicit exception that 'ships first with no eval gate.' An implementer reading Success Criteria in isolation would block R6 waiting for a pre-ship writeup that Context says should not exist.
findings.location: Success Criteria (first bullet) vs. Context (GATE TERMS paragraph, 'R6 is the one deliberate exception') vs. Acceptance Criteria AC3 ('R6's terms are ship-first')
scenarios: An implementer reads Success Criteria before Context and blocks R6 from shipping until a pre-implementation writeup exists, contradicting the explicitly stated R6 exception.
A reviewer approving R6's Gerrit CL flags absence of a pre-ship docs/experiments/ writeup as a Success Criteria violation.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Success Criteria first bullet, append: '(R6 is the sole exception: its gate terms are ship-first — deterministic rendering + validator compliance verified post-ship via R7 dogfood monitoring, per Context).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md (commit 143d5074e) into the repo (or add a permalink/rendered copy under docs/), so the §6 experiment table and R1-R7 design rationale cited as the authoritative threshold source are verifiable by child implementers and reviewers.
evidence: Plan text: 'Integrate the task-decomposition SOTA research (docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md, commit 143d5074e, ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b)'
Plan text: 'each child R1-R7 story carries its own falsifiable pass/fail thresholds (drawn from the report's section-6 experiment table) INSIDE its Acceptance Criteria'
Plan text: '§6 defines R1-R7, each with a falsifiable experiment'
ABSENCE: docs/research/ directory does not exist in the repo; search for 'task-decomposition-sota' returns zero matches across all files
ABSENCE: ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b not found via show_ticket
findings.finding: The plan's gate-row thresholds, R1-R7 design rationale, and all empirical claims cited in the Context section (3.2% post-claim edit rate, defect-class evidence on dc58/db7b/5886, §5 history mining, §6 experiment table) are explicitly derived from docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md (commit 143d5074e), but that file does not exist in the repository. Child ACs carry threshold numbers 'drawn from the report's section-6 experiment table,' making the report a load-bearing, unverifiable capability-assertion source.
findings.location: Context section — 'docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md, commit 143d5074e'; also 'the report's §5 mined this store's own history'; '§6 defines R1-R7, each with a falsifiable experiment'; 'child ACs are the authoritative gate rows'
scenarios: A child implementer building R1 needs to know the §6 gate-row thresholds (e.g. E1 kappa ≥ 0.7 is stated in the epic, but E2/E3/E5/E6 thresholds are deferred to child ACs 'drawn from the report') and cannot access the report to verify or reproduce them.
A reviewer evaluating whether a child R-story has 'demonstrably passed' its gate terms cannot independently verify that the threshold was correctly transcribed from the report's §6.

If the report's empirical baseline (3.2%, 3/8 miss rate) is wrong or inapplicable, the entire justification for running E1 ground-truth re-verification (rather than trusting existing calibration data) is undermined — but no reviewer can check this.
findings.suggested_fix: Add the SOTA report as a committed doc (e.g. docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md) — it is already referenced by commit hash, so a cherry-pick or cp from that commit suffices. If the report is intentionally external, add a stable permalink and a one-page summary of the §6 gate-row table directly in this epic's Context or a linked design doc, so the thresholds are independently checkable without repo access to the referenced commit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit ordering constraint to R7's Scope and/or AC stating it must land before any of R1/R2/R3/R4/R5 land in the packaged gate.
evidence: Parent Context: 'ORDERING: first wave after E1 is R7 + R6 (instrumentation and the no-gate move), so dogfood monitoring exists before any detection change lands'

R7 Scope: 'docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ (instrumentation job + README documenting metrics, alarm rule, and cadence). Optionally a make target or documented command; NO gate/source behavior changes.'
R7 AC contains no sequencing constraint relative to R1/R2/R3/R4/R5
findings.finding: R7 lacks any ordering constraint in its own AC or Scope to enforce landing before detection-change R-stories (R1/R2/R3/R4/R5), despite the parent Context mandating 'first wave after E1 is R7 + R6 ... so dogfood monitoring exists before any detection change lands.'
findings.location: child b1a4-af7a-4a48-465d (R7) Scope and Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: R7 is deprioritized; R1 lands first; monitoring is absent for the first month of the capability probe's production operation; a blocking-FP spike goes undetected until trust damage occurs
Implementation team treats R7 as low-priority docs-only work and batches it with later R-stories
findings.suggested_fix: Add to R7 Scope: 'Must land before R1/R2/R3/R4/R5 per epic ordering mandate.' Add to R7 AC: 'The instrumentation job is committed and the alarm rule is documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md BEFORE any detection-change R-story (R1/R2/R3/R4/R5) is merged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] R7 must either reuse/extend E1's mine_outcome_corpus.py (importing or wrapping it) or explicitly document the delta between the two implementations and why agreement at 6/8 is still sufficient given that delta.
evidence: E1 Scope: 'harnesses/mine_outcome_corpus.py (new)... Recovers history via git cat-file, reads current state via rebar show'
R7 What: 'a committed job that classifies each reviewed ticket's outcome from store history (the E1 outcome-corpus method, re-runnable on the live store)'
R7 AC1: 'The instrumentation job is committed and re-runnable against the live store'
R7 AC2: 'the job classifies the 8 §5.2 tickets with >=6/8 agreement against the E1 frozen labels'
Neither E1 nor R7 states that R7 reuses or extends mine_outcome_corpus.py; R7 re-implements 'the E1 outcome-corpus method'
findings.finding: E1's mine_outcome_corpus.py and R7's instrumentation job independently implement the same git-object outcome-classification logic with no stated reuse relationship, creating a divergence risk that directly undermines R7's reproduction gate (>=6/8 agreement with E1's frozen labels).
findings.location: child e95e-1543-cac7-47b9 (E1) Scope; child b1a4-af7a-4a48-465d (R7) What/AC
scenarios: E1's mine_outcome_corpus.py uses a slightly different post_claim_edit_class assignment rule than R7's job; the 8 §5.2 tickets classify differently; R7 fails its own reproduction gate
The two implementations diverge silently over time as store schema evolves; R7 metrics become incomparable to E1's frozen baseline
findings.suggested_fix: Update R7's Scope to state: 'The instrumentation job extends or wraps harnesses/mine_outcome_corpus.py from E1 rather than re-implementing the outcome-classification logic independently; shared classification logic lives in one place.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the in-session verification method for 'no blocking-FP regression' in the Success Criteria (e.g., cite the specific experiment writeup section, a CI eval command, or the before/after blocking-FP numbers each child's gate row must record before merge).
evidence: 'The gate ships with no blocking-FP regression: validated changes are incorporated DIRECTLY into the packaged review process'
The 10% threshold is defined only for post-deployment R7 dogfood monitoring ('when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted')
No CI gate, eval-suite command, or experiment artifact is named as the pre-ship FP check
findings.finding: The success criterion 'The gate ships with no blocking-FP regression' names no in-session verification mechanism — no CI gate, eval command, or specific experiment artifact is cited to confirm the absence of regression before merge. The 10 % demotion threshold is defined for post-deployment R7 monitoring, not for a pre-ship check, leaving an executor unable to evaluate this criterion at merge time without guessing at the method.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'The gate ships with no blocking-FP regression'
scenarios: An executor merging R2 (or R3/R4) must decide whether 'no blocking-FP regression' has been satisfied — without a named artifact or command, they cannot make this determination in-session and may either block unnecessarily or skip the check entirely.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the criterion: 'verified by each landing child's committed experiment writeup recording a blocking-FP rate no higher than the pre-change baseline measured in E1 (or E2/E3/E5/E6 as applicable).' This ties the pre-ship check to the already-required experiment artifacts.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out/rollback plan for R5's review_kernel changes (e.g., Gerrit revert procedure, or an expand-contract sequence where plan-review uses the new kernel path while code-review stays on the old path until a clean-bill-of-health window passes).
evidence: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'
'validated changes are incorporated DIRECTLY into the packaged review process (no config-flag or overlay gating)'
Absence: no back-out plan, Gerrit revert procedure, or expand-contract sequencing described for review_kernel changes post-merge
findings.finding: The plan has no explicit back-out/rollback path for landed changes to the shared review_kernel module. The E5 non-regression gate is a pre-landing gate only; if a regression in code-review is discovered post-merge, there is no described revert procedure or isolation strategy. The plan explicitly forbids config-flag gating, so no feature-flag rollback exists either.
findings.location: Context: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'
scenarios: R5 lands, passes E5 non-regression gate on eval corpus, but a code-review regression surfaces in production dogfooding — no documented path to revert without re-breaking plan-review.
A post-merge discovery that the eval corpus did not cover a real code-review pattern; the shared module is now defective for both consumers with no described isolation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit ADR or CLAUDE.md section documenting the review_kernel cross-component coupling decision (shared with code-review, non-regression gate contract, and R5's expansion), as a required deliverable of the R5 child story.
evidence: Plan states review_kernel is 'SHARED with code-review' but does not list an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry as a deliverable for this cross-component coupling
AC and Success Criteria list docs/plan-review-gate.md as the doc artifact, but this is a per-recommendation changelog, not an architectural decision record for the shared kernel boundary
Novel cross-component contract (non-regression gate, shared sub-answer kernel) has no designated ADR destination
findings.finding: The shared review_kernel cross-component coupling (plan_review and code-review share the module; R5 expands it) is acknowledged in passing but no ADR or CLAUDE.md update is called for to capture this architectural decision, leaving the rationale and interface contract undocumented for future maintainers.
findings.location: Context section: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'
scenarios: A future engineer modifying review_kernel for code-review purposes inadvertently breaks plan-review behavior, with no ADR to consult about the coupling contract
Onboarding a new contributor who cannot determine from docs alone why the shared kernel exists or what its interface guarantees are
findings.suggested_fix: In the R5 child story AC, add: 'A new ADR (docs/adr/NNN-review-kernel-shared-coupling.md) or a CLAUDE.md section is committed capturing: (a) why review_kernel is shared across plan-review and code-review, (b) the interface boundary, and (c) the E5 non-regression gate as the coupling-safety mechanism.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 10% blocking-FP proxy demotion threshold in a named, findable config location (e.g., a field in criteria_routing.json or a constants module), and reference that location in docs/plan-review-gate.md so it can be adjusted without grepping epic text.
evidence: 'exceeds 10%' appears only in the epic narrative, not in any named config artifact
The demotion action is a criteria_routing.json edit, but the 10% trigger threshold has no corresponding config key or constant that a maintainer would update if the policy changes
Precedent cited (calibration 3, T5e demotion) does not indicate the threshold was configurable either — reinforces the pattern of undocumented thresholds
findings.finding: The 10% blocking-FP proxy threshold that governs automatic posture demotion is stated only in the epic's narrative text; there is no specified config location (e.g., criteria_routing.json field, constants file) where it lives and can be adjusted, making it a de-facto hardcoded policy number.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
scenarios: After dogfooding reveals the 10% threshold is too tight or too loose, a maintainer must grep for '10%' in docs to find and change it, with no single authoritative config location
Different child stories may interpret '10%' differently if the number lives only in this epic text
findings.suggested_fix: Add a top-level field in criteria_routing.json such as `"posture_demotion_fp_threshold": 0.10` and reference it in the R7 monitoring instrumentation, with docs/plan-review-gate.md pointing to that field as the authoritative value.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a formal dependency from R5 onto the floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) so the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump train is enforced by sequencing, not a process agreement — specify which story owns the bump and whether R5 increments it again or inherits the floor-split story's bump.
evidence: Plan states: 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
Plan also states: 'R2/R3/R4 each touch criteria_routing.json and land serially in any order (file-impact keeps them out of one batch)' — this is enforced by sequencing, not a process agreement
R5 has no analogous explicit dependency on the floor-split story despite the same 'one bump train' invariant requirement

ADR 0036 (docs/adr/0036-impact-model-rollout-and-calibration-cadence.md) states: 'Bump the constant whenever the formula's shape changes; that starts a fresh calibration cohort and the segmented replay will never mix the old and new scores' — mixing depends on clean bump ordering
IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION constant lives in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:35 — a file both R5 and the floor-split story could touch
findings.finding: R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination is described as a process agreement ('coordinated at claim time') but is not enforced by a dependency-structure ordering. Unlike R2/R3/R4 (which are serially sequenced after the floor-split story to avoid file conflicts), R5 is described as independent of the floor-split story. Two concurrent-claiming agents could both bump the sidecar.py constant independently, producing a split-corpus where sidecars from the transition window are tagged with neither the old nor the new version unambiguously — defeating the segmented calibration invariant described in ADR 0036.
findings.location: Context section: 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
scenarios: Two agents simultaneously claim R5 and the floor-split story; both bump IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION to different values; merge conflict is resolved but sidecars emitted between the two merges carry the intermediate version, corrupting the calibration cohort segmentation

The floor-split story lands first and bumps the version; R5 later tries to claim its bump but the plan doesn't specify whether R5 should use the already-bumped version or make a second bump — 'never two half-bumps' is under-defined when the floor-split story already made a bump
findings.suggested_fix: Add R5's ticket dependency on large-sleepful-needlefish (mirroring how R2/R3/R4 depend on the floor-split story for criteria_routing.json). In R5's AC, specify: 'R5 does not bump IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION independently; it claims the version bumped by large-sleepful-needlefish' OR 'R5 makes the second and final bump, claimable only after large-sleepful-needlefish is closed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split the E1 AC's single kappa threshold into two per-corpus requirements: state the validity gate for the outcome corpus (kappa >= 0.7 on plan-has-defect labels) and the finding-adjudication corpus (kappa >= 0.7 on finding-is-real labels) separately, so each can independently trigger the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE.
evidence: AC names two distinct corpora: 'outcome corpus' and 'finding-adjudication corpus'
A single kappa threshold (>= 0.7) is given for both, with no per-corpus floor

The existing experiment docs (plan-review-validation-experiment-plan.md) distinguish E1's 'seeded-defect recall' (outcome validity) from finding adjudication (LLM output quality) as separate measurement axes

INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE: 'an experiment that fails its own validity gate (e.g. E1 kappa < 0.7) or returns an ambiguous result gets at most ONE documented re-run' — an experiment with 0.72 outcome-kappa / 0.55 finding-adjudication-kappa would not trigger this rule under the current AC wording
findings.finding: The E1 AC specifies 'inter-rater kappa >= 0.7 recorded' as a single threshold applied to two structurally distinct corpora (the outcome corpus — does this plan have defects? — and the finding-adjudication corpus — are these LLM-surfaced findings real?). These corpora measure different validity dimensions: outcome kappa validates the ground-truth labels the whole experiment chain depends on; finding-adjudication kappa validates the LLM's output quality. A single threshold against both is under-specified: a gate with outcome-kappa 0.72 and finding-adjudication-kappa 0.55 would formally pass the AC but leave the finding-adjudication corpus unreliable, undermining every dependent R-story's gate results.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, line 1: 'E1 ground-truth corpora are frozen and committed under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ with a versioned README (outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus, inter-rater kappa >= 0.7 recorded)'
scenarios: E1 outcome corpus achieves kappa 0.75 (raters agree on which plans are defective), but finding-adjudication corpus achieves kappa 0.52 (raters disagree on whether flagged findings are real defects). The AC as written is satisfied, but R-stories that depend on the finding-adjudication corpus to set their recall thresholds are built on an unreliable foundation.
Conversely: finding-adjudication kappa is high but outcome kappa is low — the gate result labels are noisy, invalidating the recall measurement used to set R-story thresholds.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'outcome corpus + finding-adjudication corpus, inter-rater kappa >= 0.7 recorded' with 'outcome corpus kappa >= 0.7 (inter-rater agreement on plan-level defect presence) AND finding-adjudication corpus kappa >= 0.7 (inter-rater agreement on finding validity); either below threshold triggers the INCONCLUSIVE-GATE RULE on the affected corpus.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC3, replace 'validator compliance' with a concrete proving command/check: name the specific validator(s) (e.g. `validate_move_registry` + `validate_subject`) and state the pass predicate (e.g. 'no ValueError raised on the new move entry; `_validate_subject` accepts the rendered subject for each new move').
evidence: Plan AC3 text: 'R6's terms are ship-first: deterministic rendering + validator compliance'
src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py exports validate_move_registry and validate_subject but neither is named in the AC
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py line 77: '_validate_subject = _coach.validate_subject' — exists but not specified as the compliance criterion
ABSENCE: no definition of what 'validator compliance' means as a gate-passing condition (which validator, what predicate, where result is recorded)
findings.finding: AC3's gate term for R6 — 'validator compliance' — is referenced as a ship condition but never defined: which validator(s) run, what the pass/fail predicate is, and where the result is recorded are unspecified. The code exports both validate_move_registry and _validate_subject / validate_subject, but the AC does not name either or define what 'compliance' means as a closing check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 — 'R6's terms are ship-first: deterministic rendering + validator compliance'
scenarios: An executor ships R6 by verifying only the template renders without error, skipping the subject-validator check entirely — passes 'deterministic rendering' but not 'validator compliance', yet there is no stated test to distinguish them.
A code-reviewer disagrees on what 'validator compliance' covers (validate_move_registry vs validate_subject vs both vs a new validator) and the AC is ambiguous as written.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC3's R6 gate terms as: 'deterministic rendering (the template renders without error from a locked template string + a validated subject) + validate_move_registry passes on the new entry + _validate_subject accepts every auto-generated subject in a unit test.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'trailing blocking-FP proxy' metric: specify the time window (e.g. trailing 30 reviews), the input (REVIEW_RESULT events where a finding on this criterion blocked, later adjudicated as FP), the formula (count of blocking-FP events / total blocking events for that criterion), and the data source (the R7-instrumented REVIEW_RESULT sidecar field).
evidence: Plan Success Criteria: 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory'
Search for 'trailing.*FP' and 'FP.*proxy' and 'blocking-FP' in the codebase returned no matches — metric is not defined anywhere in the repo
The T5e precedent was calibration-replay analysis (docs/experiments/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md), not a named running metric derived from REVIEW_RESULT events
ABSENCE: no formula, window size, or event-source definition for the 10% threshold trigger anywhere in the plan or codebase
findings.finding: The Success Criteria's posture-demotion trigger — 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory' — references an undefined metric. The computation of the 'trailing blocking-FP proxy' (time window, input events, formula) is never defined in the plan, the existing docs, or the codebase. The R7 instrumentation story is cited as providing the monitoring, but R7's own spec is a child ticket whose ACs are declared authoritative — creating a forward reference to an unresolved schema.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'when a criterion's trailing blocking-FP proxy exceeds 10%, its registry posture is demoted to advisory by an ordinary criteria_routing.json change'
scenarios: R7 ships and instruments the sidecar, but the demotion decision rule cannot be evaluated because the 'trailing blocking-FP proxy' has no defined computation — the 10% trigger is unactionable.
Two reviewers compute the proxy differently (different windows, different event filters) and reach different demotion decisions for the same criterion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination mechanism: name the owner (e.g. the R5 story's implementor checks the floor-split story's current status at claim time), define the check (e.g. 'verify large-sleepful-needlefish has shipped its bump before R5 bumps, or coordinate a joint bump'), and state the fallback if both are simultaneously mid-flight (e.g. 'one story defers its bump and the two ship in sequence').
evidence: Plan Context text: 'coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump'
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py line 35: IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION = 'plan-v3' — the current version stamp

docs/adr/0036-impact-model-rollout-and-calibration-cadence.md line 63: 'each IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION change resets the cohort; do not carry a...' — confirms the invariant but not the coordination mechanism
ABSENCE: no defined owner of the coordination check, no lock/flag mechanism, no fallback if both stories bump mid-flight simultaneously
findings.finding: The IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump coordination rule ('coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump') has no defined owner, check mechanism, or fallback for a concurrent mid-flight scenario. The plan states 'one bump train, never two half-bumps' as an invariant but specifies no protocol for enforcing it when both R5 and the floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) are simultaneously active.
findings.location: Context section — 'R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump is coordinated at claim time with the floor-split story's own potential bump so the corpus segments cleanly (one bump train, never two half-bumps)'
scenarios: R5 claims work and bumps IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION to 'plan-v4' concurrently with the floor-split story also preparing to bump it — two separate 'plan-v4' bumps each reset the cohort at different times, mixing corpus segments.
The coordination is done by a convention ('at claim time') with no enforcing mechanism, so it silently fails under autonomous multi-agent execution.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] E3 must specify whether candidates run in isolation (one-at-a-time) or together, and if together, define an attribution methodology that isolates per-candidate flip contribution (e.g., additive delta against a baseline-only run).
evidence: E3 What: 'Re-run the amended gate (each R-candidate as packaged registry/rubric edits on an experiment branch build)'
E3 AC2: 'per-criterion finding deltas alongside verdict flips so an R-row's churn is attributable'
R1 AC1: 'E3 PASS->BLOCK <=2%' — R1 consumes E3's flip rate as a pass/fail gate leg

If R1+R3+R4 all run simultaneously, a plan that flips PASS->BLOCK due to R3's new container criterion is counted in R1's flip-rate measurement, potentially causing R1 to fail its <=2% gate even if R1 alone would pass
findings.finding: E3 runs all R-candidates simultaneously on one experiment branch, but the per-candidate PASS->BLOCK flip rate (consumed individually by R1's <=2% gate row) may be confounded by interaction effects between simultaneously active candidates - a flip caused by R1+R3 together cannot be cleanly attributed to R1 alone.
findings.location: child def5-e68d-6c2f-413b (E3) AC2/AC3; child 59b7-69b6-e763-4a83 (R1) AC1
scenarios: E3 is run with all candidates active; a batch of container plans flip PASS->BLOCK due to R3; this inflates R1's reported flip rate above 2%; R1 closes deferred despite its own probe being within the threshold
Alternatively, compensating effects mask a true R1 regression
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC to E3 requiring that each R-candidate is run SEPARATELY against the baseline (no-candidate) run to produce per-candidate flip deltas, in addition to any combined run; or explicitly state that candidates run one at a time with baseline subtraction.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] R4 must either (a) explicitly scope the bug tier as advisory-only with no blocking path (eliminating the PASS->BLOCK risk), or (b) define a substitute eval gate for the bug tier (e.g., a retrospective sample of bug plans assessed against the tier, or a separate mutant corpus for bug-plan shapes).
evidence: R4 What: 'a lightweight bug review tier so gate-exempt bugs get at least the DET floor + necessity probe at claim (today bugs skip plan review entirely)'

R4 AC1: gate is 'necessity probe fires on 5886 plan fixture and on >=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants with <=1/10 controls flagged' — covers the probe's detection quality but not the flip rate for bug tickets
E3 What: 'Re-run the amended gate over a stratified ~200-review historical sample' drawn from E1's outcome corpus of previously-reviewed tickets
Since bugs 'skip plan review entirely' today, there are no historical bug-ticket reviews to include in E3's sample; E3 cannot measure PASS->BLOCK for the bug tier
Parent AC3: 'no detection-bearing recommendation implemented without its eval gate'
findings.finding: R4's 'lightweight bug review tier' is detection-bearing for a ticket population (gate-exempt bugs) that has never had plan reviews, so E3's counterfactual replay over ~200 historical reviews structurally cannot provide the PASS->BLOCK flip rate for this new tier - the parent AC3 'no detection-bearing recommendation implemented without its eval gate' is not satisfied for the bug-tier component.
findings.location: child 03a9-e6d1-4acc-435d (R4) What/Acceptance Criteria; child def5-e68d-6c2f-413b (E3) scope
scenarios: Bug tier lands; a class of legitimate bug plans that are routine no-ops is now blocked at plan time; no E3 flip-rate data exists to identify this regression; the 10% FP proxy threshold is first breached in production
R7 eventually catches the FP spike, but only after trust damage among bug-ticket authors
findings.suggested_fix: Add to R4's AC: 'The bug review tier adds NO blocking behavior for bug tickets (DET floor is informational-only for bugs, necessity probe is advisory); this is enforced by the lifecycle test and eliminates the need for an E3 flip-rate gate on this population.' Or add: 'A retrospective sample of >=20 historical bug plans is run through the tier and the advisory-flag rate recorded before landing.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
